CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-08-2025 Continued To Dec-03-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Ma Tiers Admitted And Documents Genuine Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 2033.280 For The Failure To Respond The Request For Admission Of Facts And Documents, And Request For Sanctions Pursuant C.C.P. Section
Off Calendar Mar-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC.'s Motion To Deem Matters Admitted And Documents Genuine Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 2033.280 For The Failure To Respond The Request For Admission Of Facts And Documents, And Request For Sanctions Pursuant C.C.P. Section.

Plaintiff Align Technology, Inc.'s motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in its Requests for Admission, Set One, to defendant Robert W. Garner is granted. Defendant failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs, Set One, is deemed admitted by Defendant, as is the genuineness of any document attached. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).)

Plaintiff also seeks sanctions. CCP 2033.280(c) further provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions but does not find the amount sought to be reasonable. The court awards $1500 in sanctions to Plaintiff, payable by Defendant Robert W. Garner. Defendant shall remit sanctions within 10 court days of entry of this order.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Response To Discovery: Interrogatories-Special Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 2030.290 And Sanctions Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 2030.290 And Section 2023.010
Off Calendar Mar-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC.'s Motion To Compel Response To Discovery: Interrogatories-Special Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 2030.290 And Sanctions Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 2030.290 And Section 2023.010.

Plaintiff Align Technology, Inc.'s motion to compel Defendant Robert W. Garner to respond to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories, Set One, is granted. Defendant failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2030.290(a).) Defendant is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, within 10 court days of entry of this order.

Plaintiff also seeks sanctions. CCP 2030.290(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to interrogatories . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." The court does not find that Defendant acted with substantial justification but does not find the amount of sanctions sought to be reasonable. The court awards $1500 in sanctions to Plaintiff, payable by defendant Robert W. Garner, within 10 court days of entry of this order.

Order signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
